danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprites filling screen space; Gofer (giant head) as final antagonist
danger_signals.meaning: Significant threat requiring adaptive combat; progression checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming fire from Bacterion forces; visual density increases with difficulty
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring evasion and positioning
Active hostile faction controlling current space sector

safety_signals.visual_form: Selection bar activation; highlighted weapon/shield options before stage entry
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency in defensive/offensive capability; preparation state

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual shield effect on Vic Viper; protective aura around spacecraft
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability window; damage absorption capacity active

safety_signals.visual_form: Up to four satellite/drone units orbiting player ship
safety_signals.meaning: Increased firepower and defensive redundancy; higher survivability state

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Organic and mechanical hybrid enemy designs; spacecraft and biological entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bacterion-controlled space sectors; visual aesthetic of technological/biological fusion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Vic Viper spaceship as player avatar
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player ship customization through weapon/shield selection; visual distinction from enemy craft

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy spawn patterns; density and variety of hostile forces; boss parade level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating difficulty through level progression; boss rush mechanic signals escalating threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gofer (giant head) as final boss; symbolic representation of empire's command structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-specific attack patterns reflecting faction's military capability and strategic intelligence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Selection bar displaying weapon configurations (four selectable schemes); shield/forcefield options highlighted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pre-stage customization state; player chooses tactical loadout before engaging sectors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real-time projectile exchanges; multiple enemy types on screen simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dynamic evasion patterns; weapon cycling based on selected configuration; shield depletion visual feedback

environmental_storytelling.example: Bacterion Empire enemies display organic-mechanical hybrid aesthetic, communicating their nature as technologically-augmented biological force
Boss rush level (boss parade/alley) consists entirely of sequential boss encounters, visually communicating escalating resistance and military hierarchy
Multiple options (satellites) orbiting Vic Viper visually represent accumulated power and tactical advantage; visual feedback for power-up effectiveness
Four distinct weapon schemes (different missile/double/laser combinations) allow players to visually manifest different tactical approaches to same scenario

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Single weapon output; limited defensive capability; vulnerable to multi-hit patterns
Maximum firepower spread; enhanced survivability; capable of sustained offensive pressure
Lower-density spawn patterns; predictable projectile timing; individual enemy threat relatively low
Complex attack patterns; screen-filling projectiles; coordinated multi-phase behavior; requires tactical adaptation
Represents peak Bacterion military capability; complex behavioral patterns reflecting empire's strategic sophistication

notes: Gradius II employs classic shmup visual communication: enemy spawn density and configuration directly signal threat level escalation; power-up system (selection bar) visually externalizes player tactical choices; boss parade level is pure world-state manifestation—the level structure itself communicates 'maximum resistance.' Faction identity relies on enemy design language (Bacterion hybrid aesthetic) rather than explicit color-coding. The Famicom NES version (1992) maintained arcade design but may have had enhanced animation through VRC4 custom mapper. Environmental storytelling is minimal—gameplay and mechanical design carry narrative weight rather than environmental dressing.
